title: Harden TTS comment preprocessor for real-world chess book PGN

source.notes: User report: Everyman Chess 'Morphy: Move By Move' PGN comments produce garbled TTS. This is the core value proposition of En Parlant~ — making annotated chess books listenable.

description: The cleanCommentForTTS() function in tts.ts handles basic SAN expansion and chess vocab, but fails on real-world patterns found in published chess book PGNs. Comments from professional annotators contain prose mixed with inline move notation, section dividers, hyphenated move ranges, and multi-line wrapping that all sound wrong when sent directly to ElevenLabs. The preprocessor needs hardening to handle these patterns.
notes: The existing cleanCommentForTTS is at tts.ts:652. expandInlineSAN is at tts.ts:594. applyChessVocab is at tts.ts:616.
All fixes go in cleanCommentForTTS or its helpers — no changes to the TTS provider code or the audio pipeline.
IMPORTANT: This function runs on every comment before TTS. It must be fast and must not break comments that already sound fine.

in_scope: Fix all the patterns below in cleanCommentForTTS and its helpers
Test against the Morphy MBM PGN as the reference case
Ensure changes don't break comments that already work well
out_of_scope: Changing the audio pipeline or caching

requirements: Pattern 1: '...h5-h4' (Black move range) → 'h5 to h4' — the '...' prefix means Black's move, the hyphen means 'to'
Pattern 2: '---' (section dividers) → remove or replace with pause. Authors use '---' as paragraph breaks in prose.
Pattern 3: '48...Be7+' (inline move with Black indicator) → '48, Bishop e7, check' — the expandInlineSAN regex doesn't match '...' as dots
Pattern 4: Multi-line comments have hard line breaks mid-sentence from 80-char PGN wrapping → join into single line before processing
Pattern 5: Semicolons before closing brace '{, followed by 48...Be7+.}' → clean trailing punctuation
Pattern 6: 'his pawn by ...h5-h4' — the standalone '...' prefix before a pawn move range
Pattern 7: Parenthetical move refs like '(45 Rxe4 variation below)' embedded in prose — should be spoken naturally
Pattern 8: '{Note that}' as a comment that's just a prose fragment linking to a variation — speak it naturally
Pattern 9: 'the h1-square' — hyphenated square reference should say 'the h1 square' not 'the h1 dash square'
Pattern 10: Single quotes around chess terms like 'wrong bishop' — strip quotes, TTS handles them poorly
edge_cases: Comment that is purely prose with no chess notation
Comment with [%eval] tags mixed with prose
Very long comment (500+ chars) — should not slow down preprocessing
Comment in a non-English language — expandInlineSAN should still work

requirements: cleanCommentForTTS must remain fast — it runs on every comment

acceptance_gates: Morphy MBM game plays through with natural-sounding narration
No garbled output for any of the 10 patterns listed above
Comments that already sound fine are not degraded

manual: Import MorphyMBM.pgn, open a game with rich commentary (game at line 898)
Navigate through the game and listen to each comment
Specifically verify: '...h5-h4' says 'h5 to h4', not garbled
Verify '---' dividers are silent (not 'dash dash dash')
Verify no quotes are audible around chess terms
Test a game from with-comments.pgn to confirm no regression (those comments are already clean)

done_criteria: All 10 patterns handled in cleanCommentForTTS or its helpers
